title: Red Trike
note: A familiar machine made monumental: chrome, rubber, red paint and memory.
alt: Painting of a bright red child's tricycle

title: Blue Trike
note: An object portrait, carefully observed and quietly theatrical.
alt: Painting of a blue vintage child's tricycle

title: Buzzy Bee
note: A beloved New Zealand object translated into the exacting language of a working drawing.
alt: Blue technical drawing showing front, side and wheel details of a Buzzy Bee pull toy

title: Green Ball
note: One green ball interrupts a field of white: a small act of graphic mischief.
alt: Drawing of golf balls with one green ball

title: Red Tee
note: Repetition, interruption and a single red signal.
alt: Drawing of golf tees with one red tee

title: Jet Plane
note: An emblem-like form floats between relic, diagram and imagined machine.
alt: Abstract cream wing-like form on a dark background

title: Monday Morning Meeting
note: Comic-book scale brings office language and heroic melodrama into collision.
alt: Pop art painting of comic characters in close-up

title: Thinking
note: A cinematic crop: identity reduced to eyes, ink and anticipation.
alt: Close-up comic painting of a figure's eyes and thought bubble

title: OMG
note: A found panel becomes a complete little theatre of looking and hesitation.
alt: Comic panel painting with a woman, man and speech bubbles

title: Blam Blam
note: A tall comic poster-panel full of cropped lettering, yellow heat and theatrical confrontation.
alt: Pop art painting of a police officer pointing a gun with repeated Blam lettering

title: With Tony
note: Studio portrait and painted portrait meet in one frame: the maker, the work, and the room around them.
alt: David Jung seated beside a large painting of Tony leaning through a car window

title: View
note: A view through a view: architecture, reflection and framing compressed into one quiet study.
alt: Painting of neighbouring rooftops seen through a long horizontal window

title: Self
note: A self-portrait mediated by screens, reflections and graphic planes.
alt: Self portrait of David Jung wearing glasses

title: Tony
note: A close portrait assembled from tactile marks and deep, warm colour.
alt: Expressive close-up portrait of a bearded man

title: Lily at Hamo's
note: A sunlit moment where the reflected world becomes part of the portrait.
alt: Portrait of a child in pink sunglasses in a field

title: The Girls
note: Portraiture as affection: poised, observant and direct.
alt: Portrait painting of a woman and a girl

title: Rabbits
note: A narrative portrait rich with posture, pattern and place.
alt: Portrait of an older woman seated with newspapers

title: Bull
note: A spare scene with the charged stillness of a film frame.
alt: Painting of a bull below a power pole in a dry field

title: Mother
note: An intimate family scene shaped by a sweeping horizontal crop and the bright interruption of red.
alt: Painting of a mother holding a baby while speaking on a cordless telephone

title: Hastings Street
note: A local streetscape sharpened into sign, silhouette and blue sky.
alt: Painting of a sunny New Zealand street scene

title: Boat
note: A small boat held in blue stillness, its reflection doubling the weight of the form.
alt: Painting of a small white rowing boat tethered on calm blue water

title: Falcon
note: A falcon crosses an expansive landscape where observation, map and memory overlap.
alt: Panoramic painting of a falcon flying above fields and a mountain range

title: Barlow
note: A three-panel telephone scene with speech bubbles, street signage and noir comic timing.
alt: Wide comic strip style painting with telephone booths, speech bubbles and close-up faces

title: Kids on Court
note: A bright court scene caught mid-action: bodies, shadows and green surface moving like choreography.
alt: Painting of three children changing shirts on a green tennis court
